instructions.instruction: The canonical vault path is always ~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault/ — check this path first on every run. If it exists (directory or symlink), use it as vault_root. If it does not exist, create it (or symlink from a custom user-chosen path).
instructions.original_snippets: **Vault discovery** — the canonical vault path is always `~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault/`. On every run, check this path first: 1. **Path exists** (directory or symlink) → use it as `vault_root`, read `tracking-database.json`. 2. **Path does not exist** → first-time setup
instructions.relevant_when: Starting any vault operation, building a tracking database, organizing talk libraries, or initializing talk processing infrastructure

instructions.instruction: For custom vault locations, create the directory at the custom path and symlink from ~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault to it. Store vault_storage_path in config for the actual path. vault_root is always the canonical path.
instructions.original_snippets: **Custom path chosen:** `mkdir -p {custom_path}` then `ln -s {custom_path} ~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault` — the symlink makes the canonical path always work. Store `vault_storage_path` as the custom path in config
instructions.relevant_when: When user wants vault data in a non-default location (e.g., Google Drive for backup)

instructions.instruction: Create tracking-database.json with empty config, talks, pptx_catalog, and confirmed_intents arrays if the file is missing
instructions.original_snippets: Create empty `tracking-database.json` with empty `config`, `talks`, `pptx_catalog`.
instructions.relevant_when: Initializing a new vault or starting work on a vault that has no tracking database

instructions.instruction: Tracking database config must contain vault_root (always ~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault), plus talks_source_dir, pptx_source_dir, python_path, template_skip_patterns
instructions.original_snippets: Set `vault_root` to `~/.claude/rhetoric-knowledge-vault` in config (always the canonical path). ... Remaining core fields: `talks_source_dir`, `pptx_source_dir`, `python_path` ... `template_skip_patterns` (default: `["template"]`).
instructions.relevant_when: Creating or validating the tracking database schema, building a talk library organizer, or initializing talk processing

instructions.instruction: Scan for new talks by globbing *.md in talks_source_dir, parse each for title, conference, date, URLs, IDs, and add with status 'pending'
instructions.original_snippets: Scan for new talks: Glob `*.md` in `talks_source_dir`. For each file not in the `talks` array, parse and add (extract title, conference, date, URLs, IDs, status `"pending"`).
instructions.relevant_when: Discovering talk metadata files, building a tracking database, inventorying talks, or organizing a talk library

instructions.instruction: Scan for .pptx files using recursive glob **/*.pptx in pptx_source_dir
instructions.original_snippets: Scan for .pptx files: Glob `**/*.pptx` in `pptx_source_dir`.
instructions.relevant_when: Cataloging presentation files for matching to talks

instructions.instruction: Skip .pptx files named *static*, *(N).pptx (conflict copies), or matching template_skip_patterns
instructions.original_snippets: Skip files named `*static*`, `*(N).pptx` (conflict copies), or matching `template_skip_patterns`. ... PPTX files named `*static*` or `*(N).pptx` are always skipped.
instructions.relevant_when: Scanning .pptx files for cataloging or extraction

instructions.instruction: Fuzzy-match .pptx files to talks by conference name and title, flagging ambiguous matches for user confirmation
instructions.original_snippets: Fuzzy-match each to a `talks[]` entry by conference + title. ... 1. Normalize conference names (strip year, 'Days', 'Conference', etc.) 2. Match by date proximity 3. Match by title substring 4. Flag ambiguous matches
instructions.relevant_when: Associating .pptx files with talk entries in the tracking database

instructions.instruction: Determine slide_source per hierarchy: 'pptx' if only pptx_path, 'pdf' if only slides_url, 'both' if both exist, 'video_extracted' if only video_url (output PDF named {youtube_id}.pdf), 'none' for transcript-only. Delete video file after extraction. If primary slides fail but video_url exists, fall back to video extraction — talk can still reach 'processed' status.
instructions.original_snippets: Set slide_source per hierarchy ... video_extracted: download 720p → ffmpeg → dedup → PDF named {youtube_id}.pdf. Delete video after. Fallback: if primary slides fail but video_url exists, fall back to video extraction.
instructions.relevant_when: Determining how to acquire slide data for a talk, implementing slide source hierarchy, or building a multi-source ingestion pipeline

instructions.instruction: A talk with no video_url is not processable — mark as 'skipped_no_sources' regardless of whether slides exist. video_url is the processability gate.
instructions.original_snippets: Mark 'skipped_no_sources' only if video_url is entirely absent — a talk with no video_url is not processable regardless of whether slides exist.
instructions.relevant_when: Filtering processable vs non-processable talks, building a tracking database, or determining talk readiness

instructions.instruction: Use yt-dlp with specific flags: --write-auto-sub --sub-lang en --skip-download --sub-format vtt
instructions.original_snippets: yt-dlp --write-auto-sub --sub-lang en --skip-download --sub-format vtt -o "{vault_root}/transcripts/{youtube_id}" "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v={youtube_id}"
instructions.relevant_when: Downloading YouTube transcripts for talk analysis

instructions.instruction: Clean VTT files: strip timestamp lines (\d{2}:\d{2}.*-->), strip cue position markers (align:start position:%), remove blank lines, deduplicate consecutive identical lines, save as .txt
instructions.original_snippets: 1. Strip all timestamp lines (lines matching `\d{2}:\d{2}.*-->`) 2. Strip cue position markers (lines like `align:start position:0%`) 3. Remove blank lines 4. Deduplicate consecutive identical lines 5. Save the cleaned text as `{vault_root}/transcripts/{youtube_id}.txt`
instructions.relevant_when: Processing VTT subtitle files into clean transcript text

instructions.instruction: Use youtube-transcript-api as fallback if yt-dlp fails
instructions.original_snippets: Use if yt-dlp fails (e.g., no auto-captions available): ... from youtube_transcript_api import YouTubeTranscriptApi
instructions.relevant_when: When primary transcript download method fails

instructions.instruction: Use gdown for Google Drive PDF slide downloads with specific command format
instructions.original_snippets: "{python_path}" -m gdown "https://drive.google.com/uc?id={google_drive_id}" -O "{vault_root}/slides/{google_drive_id}.pdf"
instructions.relevant_when: Downloading slide PDFs from Google Drive

instructions.instruction: Use the specific python-pptx extraction script from pptx-extraction.md for PPTX visual data extraction
instructions.original_snippets: Run the PPTX extraction script from `references/pptx-extraction.md` inline. This provides exact hex colors, font names, layout names, and shape types
instructions.relevant_when: Extracting visual design data from .pptx files

instructions.instruction: PPTX extraction must output JSON with per_slide_visual array (slide_number, background_color_hex, background_type, layout_name, shape_count, has_image, has_speaker_notes, shapes_summary with shape types like CLOUD_CALLOUT/EXPLOSION) and global_design object (fonts_used, background_colors, shape_types_used, color_sequence, footer_pattern). Scan directories recursively with **/*.pptx glob.
instructions.original_snippets: result = { per_slide_visual: [slide_number, background_color_hex, layout_name, shape_count, shapes_summary], global_design: { fonts_used, background_colors, shape_types_used, color_sequence } } ... Recursively glob **/*.pptx in pptx_source_dir
instructions.relevant_when: Building a PPTX visual data extraction tool, slide deck audit, or presentation analytics pipeline

instructions.instruction: Extract background color using slide.background.fill first, falling back to slide_layout.background.fill
instructions.original_snippets: def get_background_color(slide): ... bg = slide.background ... # Fall back to slide layout background ... layout_bg = slide.slide_layout.background.fill
instructions.relevant_when: Extracting background colors from PowerPoint slides

instructions.instruction: Detect footer text by position: text shapes in the bottom 15% of the slide (shape.top > slide_height * 0.85)
instructions.original_snippets: # Detect footer by position (bottom 15% of slide) and small font ... if shape.top and shape.top > prs.slide_height * 0.85: slide_data["footer_text"] = shape.text_frame.text
instructions.relevant_when: Identifying footer elements in PPTX slides

instructions.instruction: Analyze all 14 rhetoric dimensions from rhetoric-dimensions.md, including dimension 14 (Areas for Improvement)
instructions.original_snippets: Apply all 14 dimensions from `references/rhetoric-dimensions.md` (including dimension 14: Areas for Improvement).
instructions.relevant_when: Analyzing a talk for rhetoric and style patterns

instructions.instruction: Per-slide visual classification must include: background color name, content type, image composition, speech bubble present, starburst present, footer visible
instructions.original_snippets: For EACH slide, determine: Background color name ... Content type ... Image composition ... Speech bubble present? ... Starburst/explosion shape present? ... Footer visible?
instructions.relevant_when: Classifying slide visual elements during talk analysis

instructions.instruction: Record background_color_sequence for every slide in order
instructions.original_snippets: Record the background color of every slide in order as `background_color_sequence`.
instructions.relevant_when: Tracking slide color patterns across a talk

instructions.instruction: Extract structured_data with specific fields: slide_count, talk_duration_estimate, meme_count, image_only_slide_count, audience_interaction_count, opening_type, closing_type, narrative_arc_type, slide_design_style, opening_sequence, closing_sequence
instructions.original_snippets: Count and categorize these for the `structured_data` output: slide_count ... talk_duration_estimate ... meme_count ... image_only_slide_count ... audience_interaction_count ... opening_type ... closing_type ... narrative_arc_type ... slide_design_style ... opening_sequence ... closing_sequence
instructions.relevant_when: Producing structured analysis output for a talk

instructions.instruction: Extract verbatim_examples with fields: signature_phrases, jokes, transitions, audience_addresses, opening_lines, closing_lines
instructions.original_snippets: Extract ACTUAL QUOTES from the transcript for the `verbatim_examples` output: signature_phrases ... jokes ... transitions ... audience_addresses ... opening_lines ... closing_lines
instructions.relevant_when: Extracting verbatim quotes from talk transcripts

instructions.instruction: Subagent return JSON must follow the schema: filename, rhetoric_notes (500-1000 words), areas_for_improvement (100-300 words), structured_data, verbatim_examples, adherence_assessment, new_patterns, summary_updates
instructions.original_snippets: { "filename": "the .md filename", "rhetoric_notes": "500-1000 words", "areas_for_improvement": "100-300 words", "structured_data": {...}, "verbatim_examples": {...}, "adherence_assessment": "...", "new_patterns": "...", "summary_updates": "..." }
instructions.relevant_when: Producing the output of a talk analysis subagent

instructions.instruction: Error handling matrix: transcript OK + slides OK → 'processed'; transcript FAIL + slides OK → 'processed_partial'; transcript OK + slides FAIL → 'processed_partial'; both FAIL → 'skipped_download_failed'
instructions.original_snippets: Transcript | Slides | Status | Action ... OK | OK | processed | Full analysis ... FAIL | OK | processed_partial | Slides only ... OK | FAIL | processed_partial | Transcript only ... FAIL | FAIL | skipped_download_failed | Skip, move on
instructions.relevant_when: Setting status after processing a talk with download failures

instructions.instruction: Summary updates must be additive — refine and consolidate but never delete existing content
instructions.original_snippets: Be additive; refine and consolidate but never delete. ... Preserve all summary content — add/refine, never delete.
instructions.relevant_when: Updating the rhetoric-style-summary.md document

instructions.instruction: Summary sections map to profile fields: Section 1→presentation_modes, Sections 2-13→instrument_catalog, Section 15→recurring_issues, Section 16→confirmed_intents + rhetoric_defaults
instructions.original_snippets: Section 1: Presentation modes (→ profile `presentation_modes`) ... Sections 2-13: Instrument catalogs (→ profile `instrument_catalog`) ... Section 15: Areas for improvement (→ profile `guardrail_sources.recurring_issues`) ... Section 16: Speaker-confirmed intent (→ profile `confirmed_intents` + `rhetoric_defaults`)
instructions.relevant_when: Mapping rhetoric summary sections to speaker profile fields

instructions.instruction: Confirmed intents stored as JSON objects with fields: pattern, intent (deliberate|accidental|context_dependent), rule, note
instructions.original_snippets: {"pattern": "delayed_self_introduction", "intent": "deliberate", "rule": "Two-phase intro: brief bio slide 3, full re-intro mid-talk", "note": "Confirmed intentional rhetorical device"}
instructions.relevant_when: Recording speaker-confirmed design decisions

instructions.instruction: Generate speaker profile only after 10+ talks parsed AND one clarification session completed
instructions.original_snippets: When: 10+ talks parsed AND one clarification session completed. Also on explicit request.
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether to generate the speaker profile

instructions.instruction: Speaker profile must follow the schema from speaker-profile-schema.md with all sections: speaker, infrastructure, presentation_modes, design_rules, rhetoric_defaults, confirmed_intents, pacing, guardrail_sources, instrument_catalog, publishing_process
instructions.original_snippets: Generate `speaker-profile.json` per `references/speaker-profile-schema.md`: speaker ← config fields ... infrastructure ← config + template layouts ... presentation_modes ... design_rules ... rhetoric_defaults ... confirmed_intents ... pacing ... guardrail_sources ... instrument_catalog ... publishing_process
instructions.relevant_when: Generating or validating the speaker profile JSON

instructions.instruction: Speaker profile must include schema_version: 1
instructions.original_snippets: "schema_version": 1
instructions.relevant_when: Generating the speaker profile

instructions.instruction: Generate speaker badges — personalized, fun, self-deprecating achievements grounded in real vault data
instructions.original_snippets: Generate speaker badges — mine the aggregated data for ridiculous achievements, milestones, and self-aware honors. Add as `badges` section in the profile. ... Badges should be genuinely personalized to THIS speaker's quirks, not generic. Make them fun, self-deprecating, and grounded in real vault data.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating the speaker profile badges section

instructions.instruction: Auto-regenerate speaker profile after every vault update if the profile already exists
instructions.original_snippets: Auto-regenerate speaker profile (Step 6) if it already exists. Report the diff.
instructions.relevant_when: After processing a batch of talks when profile exists

instructions.instruction: Create transcripts/ and slides/ directories if missing before downloading
instructions.original_snippets: Create `transcripts/` and `slides/` dirs if missing before downloading.
instructions.relevant_when: Preparing to download transcripts or slide PDFs

instructions.instruction: Re-read tracking database before every write — it is the single source of truth
instructions.original_snippets: Re-read tracking DB before writing (single source of truth).
instructions.relevant_when: Any time the tracking database is about to be written

instructions.instruction: Process talks in parallel batches of 5 subagents
instructions.original_snippets: Per batch: launch 5 subagents in parallel, wait, run Step 4, then next batch.
instructions.relevant_when: Processing multiple talks for rhetoric analysis

instructions.instruction: After 10+ talks, start providing adherence assessments in analysis output
instructions.original_snippets: After 10+ talks, start providing adherence assessments.
instructions.relevant_when: Analyzing a talk when the vault already has 10+ talks processed

instructions.instruction: PPTX extraction output must include per-slide: slide_number, background_color_hex, background_type, layout_name, shape_count, has_text_placeholder, has_image, has_speaker_notes, text_content_preview, footer_text, shapes_summary array
instructions.original_snippets: slide_data = { "slide_number": i + 1, "background_color_hex": bg_hex, "background_type": bg_type, "layout_name": ..., "shape_count": ..., "has_text_placeholder": False, "has_image": False, "has_speaker_notes": bool(...), "text_content_preview": "", "footer_text": "", "shapes_summary": [] }
instructions.relevant_when: Structuring per-slide visual extraction output

instructions.instruction: Identify auto-shape types for speech bubbles and starbursts using MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.AUTO_SHAPE and auto_shape_type
instructions.original_snippets: if shape.shape_type == MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.AUTO_SHAPE: ... info["auto_shape_type"] = str(shape.auto_shape_type)
instructions.relevant_when: Identifying special shapes in PPTX extraction

instructions.instruction: Use specific opening_type categories: provocative_image, failure_framing, audience_poll, story, bold_claim, demo_cold_open
instructions.original_snippets: opening_type: Categorize as one of: provocative_image|failure_framing|audience_poll|story|bold_claim|demo_cold_open
instructions.relevant_when: Categorizing the opening type in structured_data

instructions.instruction: Use specific closing_type categories: summary_cta, callback, open_question, demo_finale, resource_list
instructions.original_snippets: closing_type: Categorize as one of: summary_cta|callback|open_question|demo_finale|resource_list
instructions.relevant_when: Categorizing the closing type in structured_data

instructions.instruction: Use specific narrative_arc_type categories: problem_diagnosis_solution, discovery_demo, chronological, listicle
instructions.original_snippets: narrative_arc_type: problem_diagnosis_solution|discovery_demo|chronological|listicle
instructions.relevant_when: Categorizing the narrative arc in structured_data

instructions.instruction: Use specific slide_design_style categories: comic_book, minimal_dark, demo_scaffolding, mixed
instructions.original_snippets: slide_design_style: comic_book|minimal_dark|demo_scaffolding|mixed
instructions.relevant_when: Categorizing the slide design style in structured_data

instructions.instruction: Report vault state using format: 'X processed, Y remaining. PPTX: A cataloged, B matched, C extracted.'
instructions.original_snippets: Report state: "X processed, Y remaining. PPTX: A cataloged, B matched, C extracted."
instructions.relevant_when: Reporting the current state of the vault after scanning

instructions.instruction: Load vault documents in order: rhetoric-style-summary.md (constitution), slide-design-spec.md (visual rules), speaker-profile.json (structured data)
instructions.original_snippets: Load order — read all three from vault root: ... summary: rhetoric-style-summary.md ... design: slide-design-spec.md ... profile: speaker-profile.json
instructions.relevant_when: Starting work on any presentation creation task

instructions.instruction: Freshness check: if profile generated_date < summary 'Last updated', warn about potential stale data
instructions.original_snippets: Freshness check: If `profile.generated_date < summary."Last updated"`, warn that new talks may have been parsed — offer to run 'update speaker profile' first.
instructions.relevant_when: Loading vault documents at the start of a presentation task

instructions.instruction: Expect schema_version: 1 in speaker profile, warn if higher
instructions.original_snippets: Schema version: This skill expects `schema_version: 1`. Warn if higher.
instructions.relevant_when: Loading and validating the speaker profile

instructions.instruction: If profile doesn't exist (vault has <10 talks), run in summary-only mode with fallback thresholds: 1.5 slides/min, 45% Act 1 cap, three-part close
instructions.original_snippets: If profile doesn't exist (vault has <10 talks), run in summary-only mode: ... Phase 4: Guardrails | Default thresholds: 1.5 slides/min, 45% Act 1 cap, three-part close
instructions.relevant_when: When the vault has fewer than 10 talks processed and no profile exists

instructions.instruction: Do not skip phases. Do not write content before Phase 3. Phase 2 is joint, not autonomous.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not skip phases. Do not write content before Phase 3. Phase 2 is joint, not autonomous.
instructions.relevant_when: Following the presentation creation workflow

instructions.instruction: Presentation Spec must include: Title, Thesis, Audience, Venue, Mode, Commercial intent, Must-include, Must-avoid, Co-presenter, Profanity register, Duration target, Shownotes slug
instructions.original_snippets: PRESENTATION SPEC ... Title: ... Thesis: ... Audience: ... Venue: ... Mode: ... Commercial intent: ... Must-include: ... Must-avoid: ... Co-presenter: ... Profanity register: ... Duration target: ... Shownotes slug:
instructions.relevant_when: Producing the intent distillation output in Phase 1

instructions.instruction: Mode selection comes from profile presentation_modes[], not a static list
instructions.original_snippets: The instrument menu comes from the vault, not from a static file. Read the rhetoric summary (sections 2-13) and profile `instrument_catalog` to extract available options.
instructions.relevant_when: Selecting the presentation mode during Phase 2

instructions.instruction: Slide budget from profile guardrail_sources.slide_budgets: 20min→30, 30min→45, 45min→70, 60min→90, 75min→110 slides
instructions.original_snippets: Slide budget (from `profile → guardrail_sources.slide_budgets`): 20 min | 30 ... 30 min | 45 ... 45 min | 70 ... 60 min | 90 ... 75 min | 110
instructions.relevant_when: Calculating slide budget for a presentation

instructions.instruction: Outline format: ## sections with [N min, slides X-Y] time and slide range annotations
instructions.original_snippets: ## Opening Sequence [3 min, slides 1-5] ... ## Act 1: [Title] [N min, slides X-Y]
instructions.relevant_when: Writing the presentation outline

instructions.instruction: Use typed, independently-numbered placeholders: [AUTHOR 01], [DEMO 01], [DATA 01], [SCREENSHOT 01], [MEME 01]
instructions.original_snippets: Placeholders use typed, independent numbering: `[AUTHOR 01]`, `[DEMO 01]`, `[DATA 01]`, `[SCREENSHOT 01]`, `[MEME 01]` ... Each type uses independent numbering.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing placeholder content in the outline

instructions.instruction: Meme briefs must include: Template name, Search query, Overlay text, Rhetorical function
instructions.original_snippets: [MEME 01] Template: [meme template name] Search query: "[search terms]" Overlay text: [specific text] Rhetorical function: [what argument this meme serves]
instructions.relevant_when: Creating meme placeholders in the outline

instructions.instruction: Mark callbacks explicitly: [CALLBACK: reference to {element} from slide {N}], [PROGRESSIVE LIST: ...], [RUNNING GAG: Nth appearance of {gag}]
instructions.original_snippets: [CALLBACK: reference to {element} from slide {N} — {variation}] [PROGRESSIVE LIST: {list name} gains Nth item from slide {N}] [RUNNING GAG: Nth appearance of {gag}]
instructions.relevant_when: Identifying and marking callback opportunities in the outline

instructions.instruction: Include [CUT LINE] markers for shorter slot adaptation. When auditing an outline, check for [CUT LINE] markers and flag as [FAIL] if missing — especially when the talk duration is shorter than the speaker's default.
instructions.original_snippets: ## [CUT LINE: Everything below here can be dropped for short version] ... [CUT LINE] markers for shorter slot adaptation ... [EXPAND ZONE] markers for sections that can grow ... FAIL if no [CUT LINE] marker exists
instructions.relevant_when: Building or auditing presentation outlines — both creation and quality checks

instructions.instruction: Opening sequence should include title slide, opening hook, brief bio, shownotes URL
instructions.original_snippets: ### Slide 1: Title Slide ... ### Slide 2: [Opening hook type] ... ### Slide 3: Brief Bio ... ### Slide 4: Shownotes URL
instructions.relevant_when: Structuring the opening of a presentation outline

instructions.instruction: Three-part close: summary (2-3 bullet points), CTA (specific actionable step), social (handles + shownotes URL)
instructions.original_snippets: Summary: At least 2-3 bullet points crystallizing the argument ... CTA: At least one specific, actionable next step ... Social: Speaker handles + shownotes URL
instructions.relevant_when: Structuring the closing sequence of a presentation

instructions.instruction: For co-presented talks, use [SPEAKER A]: / [SPEAKER B]: prefixes in all speaker notes
instructions.original_snippets: Use `[SPEAKER A]:` / `[SPEAKER B]:` prefixes in notes ... Voice Differentiation (use `[SPEAKER A]:` / `[SPEAKER B]:` prefixes in notes throughout the outline)
instructions.relevant_when: Creating outlines for co-presented talks

instructions.instruction: For co-presented talks, add footer adaptation per design_rules.footer.co_presented_extra
instructions.original_snippets: If co-presented, add: ... Footer Adaptation (`design_rules.footer.co_presented_extra`)
instructions.relevant_when: Handling branding for co-presented talks

instructions.instruction: Run all 10 guardrail checks, never skip a category: slide budget, Act 1 ratio, branding, profanity, data attribution, time-sensitive content, closing completeness, cut lines, anti-pattern flags, and illustrations ([SKIP] when no illustration strategy). Each check uses [PASS], [FAIL], [WARN], or [SKIP] prefix.
instructions.original_snippets: All 10 checks are mandatory — run every one, never skip a category ... [PASS/FAIL] Slide budget ... [PASS/WARN] Act 1 ratio ... [PASS/FAIL] Cut lines ... [PASS/FAIL/SKIP] Illustrations
instructions.relevant_when: Running quality checks or auditing a presentation outline

instructions.instruction: Guardrail report uses format: GUARDRAIL CHECK — {title} with [PASS/FAIL/WARN] prefix per check
instructions.original_snippets: GUARDRAIL CHECK — {talk title} — {date} ================================================ [PASS] Slide budget: {actual}/{max} ...
instructions.relevant_when: Formatting the guardrail check output

instructions.instruction: Act 1 ratio limits from profile: 20-30min→40%, 45min→45%, 60+min→50%
instructions.original_snippets: act1_ratio_limits: [{"duration_range": "20-30 min", "max_percent": 40}, {"duration_range": "45 min", "max_percent": 45}, {"duration_range": "60+ min", "max_percent": 50}]
instructions.relevant_when: Checking the problem-section balance in guardrails

instructions.instruction: Progressive-reveal slides count toward the slide budget (3 reveals = 3 slides, not 1)
instructions.original_snippets: Progressive-reveal slides count toward the budget. If you show the same chart 3 times with different bars highlighted, that's 3 slides, not 1.
instructions.relevant_when: Counting slides for budget compliance

instructions.instruction: On-slide profanity should be verbal-only by default — flag any baked-in profanity for approval
instructions.original_snippets: keep profanity verbal-only by default. On-slide profanity limits deck reuse across venues. ... If on-slide profanity is present without explicit approval, flag it
instructions.relevant_when: Auditing profanity in presentation content

instructions.instruction: Strip demo slides from template using python-pptx drop_rel pattern before building deck
instructions.original_snippets: tmpl = Presentation('{template_pptx_path}') ... xml_slides = tmpl.slides._sldIdLst ... for sldId in list(xml_slides): rId = sldId.get('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships}id') ... tmpl.part.drop_rel(rId) ... xml_slides.remove(sldId)
instructions.relevant_when: Creating a clean deck from a template for slide generation

instructions.instruction: Inject speaker notes via python-pptx batch, not MCP (MCP doesn't support notes)
instructions.original_snippets: Batch-inject via python-pptx (MCP doesn't support notes). ... The MCP PPT server does not support speaker notes. Use python-pptx directly as a batch operation after all slides are generated via MCP.
instructions.relevant_when: Adding speaker notes to a generated deck

instructions.instruction: Add footer to EVERY slide using manage_text, with position, font, and color from design_rules.footer
instructions.original_snippets: Add footer to EVERY slide using `manage_text`. The footer pattern template is in `footer.pattern` — substitute conference-specific values.
instructions.relevant_when: Adding branding footer to generated slides

instructions.instruction: Background color strategy from profile: random_non_repeating means never repeat on adjacent slides
instructions.original_snippets: design_rules.background_color_strategy ... `random_non_repeating` — pick a random saturated color, never repeat on adjacent slides
instructions.relevant_when: Selecting background colors during slide generation

instructions.instruction: White/black backgrounds reserved for full-bleed image/meme slides only
instructions.original_snippets: Read `design_rules.white_black_reserved_for` to know when white/black backgrounds are appropriate (typically full-bleed image/meme slides only).
instructions.relevant_when: Choosing background colors for slides

instructions.instruction: Slide numbers are typically 'never' per design_rules.slide_numbers
instructions.original_snippets: design_rules.slide_numbers — typically "never"
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether to add slide numbers

instructions.instruction: CFP abstract writing: complete Phase 0-1 (lighter), skip Phase 2, produce title + abstract (200-300 words) + key takeaways (3-5 bullets) + speaker bio
instructions.original_snippets: 1. Complete Phase 0-1 (lighter touch) 2. Skip Phase 2 (not needed for an abstract) 3. Write: title, abstract (200-300 words), key takeaways (3-5 bullets), speaker bio 4. Phase 4 revision as normal
instructions.relevant_when: Writing a conference call-for-papers submission

instructions.instruction: When adapting existing talks: copy previous deck as starting point, do NOT start from fresh template
instructions.original_snippets: Copy the previous deck as starting point — do NOT start from fresh template.
instructions.relevant_when: Adapting an existing talk for a new audience or venue

instructions.instruction: Adaptation checklist includes: footer, shownotes slug, time-sensitive content, slide budget, profanity register, locale references, commercial intent
instructions.original_snippets: Auto-generate adaptation checklist: footer, shownotes slug, time-sensitive content, slide budget, profanity register, locale references, commercial intent.
instructions.relevant_when: Adapting an existing talk for a new venue

instructions.instruction: Voice calibration: place verbal signatures from vault organically — don't force them
instructions.original_snippets: Read verbal signatures from the vault summary ... Place them where they fit organically — don't force them.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing speaker notes in the speaker's voice

instructions.instruction: Bullet symbols: read default from design_rules.default_bullet_symbol, but suggest contextual symbols where they fit
instructions.original_snippets: Bullet symbols — read default from `design_rules.default_bullet_symbol` in the profile, but proactively suggest contextual symbols where they fit
instructions.relevant_when: Choosing bullet point symbols in slide content

instructions.instruction: Save outline to: {presentations-dir}/{conference}/{year}/{talk-slug}/presentation-outline.md
instructions.original_snippets: Save to: `{presentations-dir}/{conference}/{year}/{talk-slug}/presentation-outline.md`
instructions.relevant_when: Saving the finished presentation outline

instructions.instruction: Publishing workflow read from profile publishing_process: export, shownotes, QR code, additional steps
instructions.original_snippets: Execute the steps from the profile: 1. Export — run `export_method` / `export_script` 2. Shownotes — if `shownotes_publishing.enabled` 3. QR Code — if `qr_code.enabled` 4. Additional steps — execute each `additional_steps[]`
instructions.relevant_when: Publishing a completed presentation

instructions.instruction: Data slides must have visible source attribution — flag orphaned percentages without context
instructions.original_snippets: Source name visible ... No orphaned percentages without context ("84% of developers..." — 84% of what survey?)
instructions.relevant_when: Checking data attribution in guardrail audit

instructions.instruction: Keep file versions: before major revisions, create a timestamped copy; active outline is always presentation-outline.md
instructions.original_snippets: Keep file versions: Before major revisions, create a timestamped copy ... The current/active outline is always `presentation-outline.md`
instructions.relevant_when: Iterating on presentation revisions

instructions.instruction: Illustration Strategy (Decision #11) is optional — only when the author wants AI-generated illustrations. Present 3-4 style options informed by the talk's concepts, the vault's visual_style_history, and mode-specific visual precedent
instructions.original_snippets: Decision #11: Illustration Strategy (when applicable) ... Present 3-4 style options informed by three sources: 1. The talk's own concepts ... 2. The vault's visual history — read visual_style_history ... 3. Historical precedent for this mode/context
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 2 architecture when the author wants AI-generated illustrations

instructions.instruction: Each illustration style option must explain WHY it fits the talk's concepts (CONCEPT FIT) and how it relates to the speaker's visual history (VAULT CONTEXT), not just what it looks like
instructions.original_snippets: Each option includes: a name, why it fits this talk's concepts, how it relates to the speaker's visual history (continuation vs. departure), and a sample prompt excerpt ... The key: each style option explains WHY it fits this specific talk's concepts, not just what it looks like
instructions.relevant_when: Proposing illustration style options during Phase 2

instructions.instruction: When illustration strategy is defined, outline header must include an Illustration Style Anchor section with Model, per-format STYLE ANCHOR sub-headings with blockquoted anchor paragraphs, and Conventions
instructions.original_snippets: ## Illustration Style Anchor ... **Model:** `[model-name]` ... ### STYLE ANCHOR (FULL — Landscape 1920×1080) > [style anchor paragraph] ... ### Conventions
instructions.relevant_when: Writing the outline header for an illustrated talk

instructions.instruction: Every slide in an illustrated outline must have a Format field (FULL, IMG+TXT, EXCEPTION). EXCEPTION slides must include a justification. Image prompts use [STYLE ANCHOR] token referencing the header anchors
instructions.original_snippets: Format is required for every slide ... EXCEPTION slides must include a justification ... Image prompt uses [STYLE ANCHOR] as a token referencing the header
instructions.relevant_when: Writing per-slide fields in an illustrated outline

instructions.instruction: Image prompt must be richer than the Illustration description — the prompt includes composition details, labeling, specific visual elements that the human-readable description omits
instructions.original_snippets: Illustration is the human-readable intent; Image prompt is the machine-readable generation input ... no prompts that are just the illustration description copy-pasted (the prompt should be richer/more specific)
instructions.relevant_when: Writing image prompts for illustrated slides

instructions.instruction: Illustration coverage guardrail (#10) only runs when the outline has an Illustration Style Anchor section. When no illustration strategy exists, output [SKIP] Illustrations in the guardrail report. When illustration strategy IS present, check: coverage ratio, format tags, EXCEPTION justification, style anchor reference, prompt quality.
instructions.original_snippets: Illustration Coverage (only runs when illustration strategy is defined) ... When no illustration strategy exists, output [SKIP] Illustrations ... [PASS/FAIL] Illustration coverage ... [PASS/FAIL] Format tags ... [WARN] EXCEPTION slides
instructions.relevant_when: Running guardrail checks on any presentation outline — determines whether illustration checks run or show [SKIP]

instructions.instruction: Use [IMAGE NN] placeholder for EXCEPTION slides needing real assets. Replaces [SCREENSHOT NN] in illustration-aware outlines
instructions.original_snippets: [IMAGE 01: description — what real asset is needed] ... For EXCEPTION slides that need real photos/screenshots/data. This replaces [SCREENSHOT NN] for illustration-aware outlines
instructions.relevant_when: Writing placeholders for real assets in illustrated outlines

instructions.instruction: Dimension 13f analyzes illustration and image style: image source types, illustration aesthetic, visual coherence, style anchor evidence, visual continuity devices, image-to-slide ratio, and mode correlation
instructions.original_snippets: ### 13f. Illustration & Image Style ... image source types ... illustration aesthetic ... visual coherence ... style anchor evidence ... visual continuity devices ... image-to-slide ratio ... mode correlation
instructions.relevant_when: Analyzing a talk's slide visual style during vault ingress

instructions.instruction: Structured data extraction includes illustration_style, illustration_coherence, image_source_distribution, and visual_continuity_devices fields
instructions.original_snippets: illustration_style: Name the dominant illustration aesthetic ... illustration_coherence: unified, mixed, none ... image_source_distribution: Object mapping source types to counts ... visual_continuity_devices: List any recurring visual motifs
instructions.relevant_when: Extracting structured data from a talk's visual analysis

instructions.instruction: Speaker profile includes visual_style_history with default_illustration_style, style_departures, mode_visual_profiles, and confirmed_visual_intents
instructions.original_snippets: visual_style_history: { default_illustration_style ... style_departures ... mode_visual_profiles ... confirmed_visual_intents }
instructions.relevant_when: Generating or reading the speaker profile for visual style data

instructions.instruction: Build specifications use backwards-chaining: start from the full slide image and remove one element at a time working backwards to build-00 (empty frame). Never build up from empty.
instructions.original_snippets: **Backwards-chaining workflow:** 1. Start from the full slide image ... 2. Use image editing to remove the last element → that's build step N-1 ... This backwards approach produces better results than building up from empty
instructions.relevant_when: Generating progressive-reveal build images for illustrated slides

instructions.instruction: Build-00 is always the empty frame (title/borders only, no content). The final build step is tagged [FULL] and is a copy of the full slide image.
instructions.original_snippets: - `build-00` is always the empty frame (title/borders only, no content) ... - The final build step is tagged `[FULL]` — it's a copy of the full slide image
instructions.relevant_when: Writing build specifications in presentation outlines

instructions.instruction: Build slides are inserted as separate slides in the deck (not PowerPoint animations). All use BLANK layout with full-bleed manage_image positioning. Speaker notes go ONLY on the final build step.
instructions.original_snippets: Build slides are inserted as separate slides in the deck (not PowerPoint animations) ... All build slides use the BLANK layout with `manage_image` full-bleed positioning ... Speaker notes go ONLY on the final build step
instructions.relevant_when: Inserting progressive-reveal build images into a PowerPoint deck

instructions.instruction: Build slides count toward the slide budget. The parent slide is replaced by its build sequence (not duplicated after it).
instructions.original_snippets: Build slides count toward the slide budget ... The final build step (build-N) is visually identical to the parent slide. In the deck, the parent slide is replaced by its build sequence (not duplicated after it)
instructions.relevant_when: Calculating slide budget when outline has builds, and inserting builds into the deck

instructions.instruction: For content additions (new elements, changed composition), regenerate from the full prompt. Image editing strips the style when adding new content. For content removal (erase a label, remove an element), image editing works well — the model preserves style when removing.
instructions.original_snippets: **Content additions** ... → regenerate from the full prompt. Image editing often strips the style when adding new content. ... **Content removal** ... → image editing works well. The model preserves style when removing, but not when adding.
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether to regenerate or edit an existing illustration

instructions.instruction: Never simplify original prompts. The specificity IS what makes the style work. Only append to the anchor, never trim.
instructions.original_snippets: **Never simplify original prompts.** The specificity IS what makes the style work. ... Only append to the anchor (e.g., "large bold font", "WWII uniforms"), never trim.
instructions.relevant_when: Writing or editing illustration prompts for regeneration

instructions.instruction: Always include 'DO NOT add any new elements' in edit prompts. Always include 'let background continue naturally -- no parchment patch' when erasing. Include explicit preservation instructions ('keep the road', 'keep the soldiers').
instructions.original_snippets: Always include "DO NOT add any new elements" — models love adding frames ... Always include "let background continue naturally -- no parchment patch" when erasing ... Include explicit preservation instructions: "keep the road", "keep the soldiers"
instructions.relevant_when: Writing edit or fix prompts for existing illustrations

instructions.instruction: Save v2, v3, v4 instead of overwriting during iteration. Use --fix for targeted corrections to near-perfect images (90%+ correct). Never use PIL or programmatic image masking for builds.
instructions.original_snippets: Save v2, v3, v4 instead of overwriting — iterate on the best version ... For near-perfect images (90%+ correct), use `--fix` rather than full regeneration ... Never use PIL or programmatic image masking for builds — mismatched textures look terrible
instructions.relevant_when: Iterating on illustration quality, generating builds

instructions.instruction: Flag edit prompts that remove content without explicit preservation instructions. Flag edit prompts missing 'DO NOT add any new elements'. Flag prompts that appear to be simplified versions of the style anchor.
instructions.original_snippets: Flag edit/fix prompts that reference removing or changing content without explicit preservation instructions ... Flag edit prompts missing "DO NOT add any new elements" ... Flag prompts that appear to be simplified or rewritten versions of the style anchor
instructions.relevant_when: Running guardrail checks on an illustrated outline with edit/fix prompts

instructions.instruction: Build coverage guardrail: check that all defined build steps have corresponding images. Report as [PASS/SKIP] Build coverage: {N} slides have builds defined, {M} build-step images generated.
instructions.original_snippets: [PASS/SKIP] Build coverage: {N} slides have builds defined, {M} build-step images generated ... SKIP if no slides define builds. PASS if all defined build steps have corresponding images
instructions.relevant_when: Running guardrail checks on an illustrated outline that has build specifications

instructions.instruction: Use checklist progression with a shared base image edited to add checkmarks across the talk. Use image editing (not regeneration) to preserve visual consistency. Track which slide is the base image.
instructions.original_snippets: **Checklist progression** — a shared base image that gets edited to add checkmarks, fill-ins, or stamps across the talk. Use image editing (not regeneration) to preserve visual consistency. Track which slide is the base image
instructions.relevant_when: Planning visual continuity devices for illustrated talks with recurring visual elements

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use generate-qr.py for QR generation. NEVER call the qrcode library directly. The script handles URL shortening, custom back-half, tracking DB updates, color matching, and secrets. Use --png-only mode when no deck is needed.
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS use `generate-qr.py` for QR code generation. NEVER call the `qrcode` library directly or generate QR images through any other means.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating a QR code for any purpose — deck insertion, standalone PNG, or any other format

instructions.instruction: The bitly/rebrandly custom back-half MUST be the talk slug. Never use a random shortener hash. The script uses talk_slug as the back-half by default.
instructions.original_snippets: The custom back-half MUST be the talk slug. Random hashes are untraceable and unprofessional. The back-half IS the slug.
instructions.relevant_when: Creating shortened URLs for QR codes or shownotes

instructions.instruction: QR always encodes the shortener URL (bit.ly/rebrandly), not the raw shownotes URL. The shortener is the decoupling layer — if shownotes URL changes, update the shortener target, printed QR stays valid. Only exception: shortener explicitly set to 'none'.
instructions.original_snippets: The QR code MUST encode the bit.ly/rebrandly URL, never the raw shownotes URL. The shortener is the decoupling layer.
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding which URL to encode in a QR code

instructions.instruction: When shortener key is missing from speaker profile (not set to 'none'), STOP and ask the user to choose one. Missing config is NOT intentional opt-out. Do not silently fall back to raw URL.
instructions.original_snippets: Missing Config ≠ Intentional Opt-Out. 'shortener': 'none' → explicit opt-out. shortener key missing → NOT CONFIGURED → ask.
instructions.relevant_when: Starting QR generation workflow when shortener config may be incomplete

instructions.instruction: If secrets.json is missing the required API token, STOP and guide the user to create it. Do not silently degrade to raw URL. The script prints actionable creation commands.
instructions.original_snippets: If secrets.json is missing or lacks the required API token, STOP and guide the user to create it. Do not silently degrade to a raw URL.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating QR codes or illustrations when API keys may be missing

instructions.instruction: Shownotes slug convention from profile config: {YYYY-MM-DD}-{conference-slug}-{talk-short-name}. Derive mechanically from delivery date + conference + title. Confirm with author in Phase 1. Save in presentation-spec.md.
instructions.original_snippets: Slug format: {YYYY-MM-DD}-{conference-slug}-{talk-short-name}. Derive mechanically from delivery date + conference name + talk title. Kebab-case, lowercase, no special characters.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating a talk slug during Phase 1 or constructing shownotes URLs in Phase 6

instructions.instruction: Save the Presentation Spec to presentation-spec.md alongside the outline after Phase 1. This file is the source of truth for slug, duration, mode. Later phases read it from disk.
instructions.original_snippets: Save to: {presentations-dir}/{conference}/{year}/{talk-slug}/presentation-spec.md. This file is the source of truth for the talk slug, duration, mode, and other metadata.
instructions.relevant_when: Completing Phase 1 spec, or reading talk metadata in any later phase

instructions.instruction: Gemini API key is read from {vault}/secrets.json (gemini.api_key) first, falling back to GEMINI_API_KEY env var. Use --vault to specify a custom vault path.
instructions.original_snippets: API key resolution order: 1. {vault}/secrets.json → gemini.api_key. 2. GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable (backward compat).
instructions.relevant_when: Running generate-illustrations.py or any Gemini API operation

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use extract-resources.py for initial resource extraction from the outline. Do not manually scan the outline first.
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS use `extract-resources.py` for initial resource extraction. Do not manually scan the outline first — the script handles URL detection, repo matching, book patterns, RFC citations, and tool mentions consistently.
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 6 Step 6.0 resource gathering, preparing resources for shownotes

instructions.instruction: The speaker MUST review extracted resources before publishing. The extracted list is candidates, not final. Never auto-publish without explicit speaker approval.
instructions.original_snippets: The extracted list is **candidates**, not the final resource list. The speaker MUST review, approve, edit, or remove items before any publishing step uses them. Never auto-publish extracted resources without explicit speaker approval.
instructions.relevant_when: After running extract-resources.py, before shownotes publishing

instructions.instruction: Coda section resources get priority — sort them higher in the review list and flag as 'from Coda section'.
instructions.original_snippets: Resources from Coda slides (closing/further-reading sections) are the most intentional — the speaker deliberately chose to surface them. Sort these higher in the review list and flag them as 'from Coda section.'
instructions.relevant_when: Presenting extracted resources for speaker review

instructions.instruction: resources.json lives in the talk working directory alongside presentation-outline.md, not vault-level.
instructions.original_snippets: `resources.json` lives in the talk working directory alongside `presentation-outline.md` and `presentation-spec.md`. It is talk-specific, not vault-level.
instructions.relevant_when: Saving or reading resources.json

instructions.instruction: When shownotes are enabled, Step 6.1 reads resources.json for the resource links section. Only approved:true items are included. Do not re-scan the outline.
instructions.original_snippets: If `resources.json` exists and has `approved: true` items, include a 'Resources' section with those links. Read from `resources.json` in the talk working directory (produced by Step 6.0) — do not re-scan the outline.
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 6 Step 6.1 shownotes generation

instructions.instruction: Phase 6 Step 6.0 (Resources) must happen before Step 6.1 (Shownotes). Resources extraction is step 0 in the publishing workflow.
instructions.original_snippets:
0. **Resources** — extract and curate resource list from outline (`extract-resources.py`)
1. **Export** — ...
2. **Shownotes** — if `shownotes_publishing.enabled`, use curated resources from Step 6.0
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 6 publishing workflow execution

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use generate-thumbnail.py for thumbnail generation. No hand-rolled Gemini calls or PIL composition.
instructions.original_snippets: ALWAYS use `generate-thumbnail.py` — no hand-rolled Gemini calls or PIL composition. The script encapsulates the researched prompt strategy, face preservation, and YouTube spec compliance.
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 7 Step 7.1 thumbnail generation

instructions.instruction: YouTube thumbnail specs are non-negotiable: 1280x720 pixels, 16:9, <2MB, PNG/JPG. Text must be readable at 160x90 pixels.
instructions.original_snippets: 1280x720 pixels, 16:9, <2MB, PNG or JPG. Text must be readable at 160x90 pixels (YouTube search result size). The script validates dimensions and file size automatically.
instructions.relevant_when: Generating or validating YouTube thumbnails

instructions.instruction: Slide selection for thumbnails is collaborative: agent suggests 3-5 candidates ranked by visual impact, speaker picks. Never auto-select.
instructions.original_snippets: The agent suggests 3-5 candidate slides ranked by visual impact ... **Never auto-select** — the speaker picks
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 7 Step 7.1 slide selection for thumbnail

instructions.instruction: Thumbnail slide ranking: Illustrations > bold claims > diagrams > text slides. Avoid: bio slides, shownotes URL slides, bullet-heavy slides.
instructions.original_snippets: **Illustrations** — highest impact ... **Bold claims** ... **Key diagrams** ... Avoid: bio slides, shownotes URL slides, bullet-heavy slides, generic titles.
instructions.relevant_when: Ranking slide candidates for thumbnail generation

instructions.instruction: Speaker photo for thumbnails is required and must be real (never AI-generated). Resolution: profile speaker_photo_path first, then ask user.
instructions.original_snippets: Real photo only — never AI-generated. Thumbnails with faces get 35-50% higher click-through rates. ... Resolution order: 1. `publishing_process.thumbnail.speaker_photo_path` from profile 2. Ask the user
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 7 Step 7.1 gathering thumbnail inputs

instructions.instruction: Thumbnail title text must be 5 words maximum — a hook, not the full talk title. Speaker confirms before generation.
instructions.original_snippets: This is a HOOK, not the full talk title. Bold sans-serif, thick outline/shadow. ... The speaker confirms the text before generation.
instructions.relevant_when: Proposing thumbnail title text

instructions.instruction: The Gemini prompt for thumbnails MUST include face preservation language: 'Maintain exact facial features from reference' and 'Do not stylize, beautify, or idealize.'
instructions.original_snippets: The Gemini prompt MUST include: 'Maintain exact facial features, bone structure, skin texture, and natural appearance from the reference' and 'Do not stylize, beautify, alter, or idealize the face'
instructions.relevant_when: Constructing the thumbnail generation prompt

instructions.instruction: When the speaker requests thumbnail changes, iterate on specific prompt components (expression, position, colors, text) rather than regenerating from scratch.
instructions.original_snippets: When the speaker requests changes, modify specific prompt components (expression, position, colors, text) rather than regenerating from scratch. Gemini's conversational refinement produces better results than cold restarts.
instructions.relevant_when: Revising a thumbnail after speaker feedback

instructions.instruction: Phase 7 is triggered separately — days or weeks after delivery. Not part of linear Phase 0-6 flow.
instructions.original_snippets: Triggered separately — days or weeks after delivery. Not part of the linear Phase 0-6 flow.
instructions.relevant_when: When the user asks for post-event tasks (thumbnail, video to shownotes)

instructions.instruction: Phase 7 late entry requires the same 4 files as Phase 6 plus a YouTube video URL from the speaker.
instructions.original_snippets: **Phase 7 late entry** requires the same 4 files plus a YouTube video URL from the speaker.
instructions.relevant_when: Starting Phase 7 post-event workflow

instructions.instruction: If shownotes don't exist and Step 7.2 (Video to Shownotes) is requested, STOP and ask. Either run Phase 6 Step 6.1 first or get the URL manually.
instructions.original_snippets: If shownotes don't exist and Step 7.2 is requested, STOP and ask — either run Phase 6 Step 6.1 first or get the shownotes URL manually.
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 7 Step 7.2 when shownotes are missing

instructions.instruction: Slide image resolution chain for thumbnails: existing illustration from illustrations/ dir (preferred) then PPTX extraction via generate-thumbnail.py --extract-slide then ask user for screenshot.
instructions.original_snippets: **Existing illustration** (preferred) — check `illustrations/` directory ... **PPTX extraction** — use the helper mode: python3 generate-thumbnail.py --extract-slide deck.pptx 15 ... **Ask user** — if extraction fails
instructions.relevant_when: Obtaining the slide image for thumbnail generation

instructions.instruction: Apply speaker thumbnail preferences from publishing_process.thumbnail: style_preference to --style, title_position to --title-position, brand_colors to --brand-colors.
instructions.original_snippets: Apply speaker preferences from `publishing_process.thumbnail`: `style_preference` → `--style`, `title_position` → `--title-position`, `brand_colors` → `--brand-colors`
instructions.relevant_when: Constructing the generate-thumbnail.py command

instructions.instruction: Step 7.2 reads publishing_process.video_publishing from profile for embed_method (youtube_embed, link_only, both) and shownotes_video_section.
instructions.original_snippets: Read `publishing_process.video_publishing` from speaker profile: `enabled`, `embed_method` — `youtube_embed`, `link_only`, or `both`, `shownotes_video_section`
instructions.relevant_when: Phase 7 Step 7.2 adding video to shownotes

instructions.instruction: Use insert-placeholder-slides.py to insert visually loud yellow placeholder slides at specified positions when adapting existing decks for new venues.
instructions.original_snippets: Insert bright-yellow placeholder slides into a PowerPoint deck at specified positions. Placeholders are visually loud (yellow background, bold [PLACEHOLDER] title) so they stand out in thumbnail view when editing large decks.
instructions.relevant_when: Adapting an existing talk by adding new slide positions to an existing deck

instructions.instruction: insert-placeholder-slides.py uses 1-indexed positions (final slide number after all insertions) and supports JSON batch mode or --at inline mode.
instructions.original_snippets: Positions are 1-indexed (final slide number after all insertions). ... insert-placeholder-slides.py <deck.pptx> <positions.json> ... insert-placeholder-slides.py <deck.pptx> --at 5 --title "New Slide"
instructions.relevant_when: Specifying positions for placeholder slide insertion
